skill.name: commit-chat-push
skill.description: Commit repository changes with a redacted Codex session transcript, then push the branch after review.
skill.summary: Commit code, attach redacted Codex provenance, and push the branch.
skill.author: bertona88

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command, filesystem, and sensitive-data patterns. Most high-volume hits are Markdown transcript records, but the skill intentionally reads Codex session files and can commit a transcript to a remote repository. No prompt injection or confirmed malicious intent was found, but publication should carry a privacy warning.

medium_findings.title: Codex Session Transcript May Be Published
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE. The workflow exports local Codex sessions, stages the transcript, and pushes it with code. The skill warns that sessions may contain system instructions, full outputs, and secrets, but accidental disclosure remains possible.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The stated workflow commits a redacted transcript and pushes the branch. The same files acknowledge that raw sessions can contain sensitive material.

medium_findings.title: Redaction Can Be Weakened By Export Options
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE WITH LEGITIMATE USE. The exporter defaults to safer settings, but options allow full command output and disabled redaction. These options can expose terminal output, local paths, or secrets if used without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The risky switches are explicit and semantic impact is clear. They are optional and documented, so this is a warning rather than evidence of malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Hidden Codex Session Directory Access
low_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE BUT EXPECTED. The exporter reads the configured Codex sessions directory under CODEX_HOME or the home directory. This is necessary for the skill, but users should understand that private local session data is processed.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly reads JSONL session files from the configured sessions root. The behavior matches the product purpose and has no direct network call in the script.

low_findings.title: Static Command Hits In Historical Transcript
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE FOR EXECUTION RISK. Many severe static hits occur inside a committed Markdown transcript that records past tool calls. The transcript contains command text, but it is not executable code by itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are fenced transcript records, not script logic. They still demonstrate why transcript review is needed before publication.

dangerous_patterns.title: Transcript Exfiltration Path
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill combines local session export with git commit and push. This is intended behavior, but it creates a path from private local chat data to a remote repository.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The workflow explicitly exports, stages, commits, and pushes transcript content. It includes review guidance that reduces but does not eliminate the risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Optional Full Tool Output Capture
dangerous_patterns.description: The exporter can include command outputs when tool-output is not none. Terminal output may contain secrets, raw logs, or private paths.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code path is explicit and affects exported content. The default is safer, which lowers the severity.

content.user_title: Commit Code with a Codex Transcript
content.value_statement: Teams often lose the reasoning behind a code change. This skill exports a redacted Codex transcript, commits it with the change, and pushes the branch.
actual_capabilities: Guides review of git status, diffs, staged files, and branch state.
Exports Codex JSONL sessions into Markdown transcripts.
Redacts common secret patterns and shortens home-directory paths.
Includes tool calls, commands, command status, and visible chat messages.
Stages the transcript with selected code changes for one commit.
Pushes the current branch using normal git upstream behavior.
limitations: Redaction is pattern-based and cannot guarantee removal of every secret.
The workflow requires access to local Codex session files.
Command output is omitted by default and must be enabled deliberately.
It does not resolve merge conflicts or failed push policies automatically.

use_cases.title: Preserve Feature Provenance
use_cases.description: Commit the code change and a readable transcript that explains how the change was produced.

use_cases.title: Review AI-Assisted Work
use_cases.description: Attach a concise Codex session record so reviewers can inspect prompts, actions, and verification steps.

use_cases.title: Document Delivery Evidence
use_cases.description: Keep a source-controlled record of implementation context for audits, handoffs, and release notes.

prompt_templates.title: Commit Current Work
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for a clean branch.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use commit-chat-push to review my changes, export the Codex transcript, commit both, and push the branch.

prompt_templates.title: Commit With Careful Staging
prompt_templates.scenario: There may be unrelated files in the worktree.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use commit-chat-push, but stage only the files related to this task and the exported transcript.

prompt_templates.title: Export A Specific Session
prompt_templates.scenario: The newest session may not be the right one.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use commit-chat-push with a specific Codex session path, then commit the transcript with these changes.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Before Push
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow with privacy review before publishing.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use commit-chat-push, scan the transcript for secrets, show the staged summary, then push only after the review passes.

output_examples.input: Commit this fix and include the Codex chat transcript.
output_examples.output: The assistant reviews diffs, exports a redacted transcript, stages the intended files, creates a commit, and pushes the branch.

output_examples.input: Save the chat that produced this feature before pushing.
output_examples.output: The assistant writes a Markdown transcript under the repository documentation folder and includes it with the feature commit.

output_examples.input: Push this branch, but do not include unrelated local changes.
output_examples.output: The assistant checks status, stages explicit paths only, confirms the staged diff, commits, and uses normal git push behavior.

best_practices: Review the generated transcript before staging it.
Keep command output disabled unless it is needed for audit context.
Use explicit git pathspecs when unrelated files are dirty.
anti_patterns: Do not commit raw Codex JSONL session files by default.
Do not use full tool output when commands may have printed secrets.
Do not force-push unless the user explicitly requests it.

faq.question: Does this skill push to a remote repository?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides Codex to push after review, commit, and transcript export.

faq.question: Does it commit raw Codex session files?
faq.answer: No. It prefers a redacted Markdown transcript and warns against raw JSONL logs.

faq.question: Can redaction miss secrets?
faq.answer: Yes. Redaction is a helper, so users should scan the transcript before committing.

faq.question: What tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Can I choose a specific session?
faq.answer: Yes. The exporter supports selecting a specific Codex JSONL session path.

faq.question: Is it safe for private repositories?
faq.answer: It can be used privately, but teams should approve transcript retention and secret scanning rules first.
